description: A multi-faceted platform designed to connect local communities. It consists of a primary user-facing application, a separate administrative dashboard, and a portal for national advertisers, all powered by a single, shared Firebase backend.

services.name: Firestore
services.description: Primary NoSQL database for all application data, including users, communities, businesses, posts, and more. Data structure is defined in 'docs/backend.json'.

services.name: Firebase Authentication
services.description: Manages user identity across all applications using email/password and social providers. Custom claims are used for role-based access control.

services.name: Cloud Storage for Firebase
services.description: Used for storing user-uploaded assets like profile pictures, post images, and business logos.

applications.name: Main Community Hub App
applications.description: The primary user-facing Progressive Web App (PWA) where community members, business owners, and leaders interact. Contains all core features like feeds, shopping, chat, and dashboards.
applications.notes: This is the core application that the majority of users interact with.

applications.name: Admin Dashboard
applications.description: A separate, secure application for platform administrators to manage users, communities, content, and system-wide settings. It has elevated permissions to perform backend operations.
applications.notes: This application interfaces directly with the Firebase Admin SDK for privileged operations and is not intended for public access.

applications.name: National Advertiser Dashboard
applications.description: A dedicated portal for national advertisers to create and manage their platform-wide advertising campaigns, view analytics, and manage billing.
applications.notes: Provides a focused experience for advertisers, separate from the main community application features.
